briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon. I will now begin the climate briefing for March 4, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Global Climate Crisis Warnings and Worsening Impacts
sections.content: Today, warning bells about the severity of climate change rang out across the globe. Greenlandic fishermen are facing direct threats to their livelihoods due to 'too warm' water temperatures [38, 55, 58, 59, 61], and Somali migrants displaced by climate change are encountering new challenges [37], indicating that the issue of climate refugees is becoming a reality. Furthermore, coastal and mountain tourist destinations are already suffering damage from climate change [46], and new microbial communities residing in water and soil are spreading with climate change, raising growing concerns for public health [52]. Recent research also suggests that global sea-level rise figures may have been underestimated, once again highlighting the urgency of the climate crisis [7, 66].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Domestic Climate Policies and Calls for Stronger Corporate Responsibility
sections.content: Domestically, discussions on transitioning to a carbon-neutral society are gaining momentum as the agenda and composition of the citizen representative group for public discourse on the '2050 Carbon Neutrality' pathway were finalized today [8, 19]. However, the industrial sector has been put on high alert due to government pressure to raise the 2030 carbon neutrality target [5], and with Hyundai Motor Company being criticized for formally operating its climate governance and lacking risk management capabilities [3], calls for responsible corporate roles are increasing. Furthermore, while Jeju Island is discussing commitments and efforts to create a safe and sustainable island from the climate crisis [2], concerns are also being raised that the government's push for a revised Renewable Portfolio Standard (RPS) could hinder the achievement of renewable energy supply targets [31].

sections.title: 🌱 Climate Technology Innovation and Accelerated Establishment of Carbon-Neutral Ecosystems
sections.content: Efforts to innovate technology and build ecosystems for climate crisis response are active. The Ministry of Climate, Energy and Environment and the Ministry of SMEs and Startups have launched the 'Climate Tech Innovation Alliance for Growth-Oriented Carbon Neutrality' [10, 20], and aim to establish a carbon-neutral ecosystem encompassing everything from technological innovation to commercialization through dedicated funds and the creation of demonstration complexes [6, 13, 16]. Domestically, specific technology development cases have been reported, such as powdery mildew response technology to reduce crop damage [1] and securing a patent for land-based seaweed farming technology, which could be a new breakthrough for the seaweed industry amidst the climate crisis [4]. Internationally, forest health assessment and climate change tracking using lasers and drones [49], and scientists' efforts to restore lost forests [50] are underway.

sections.title: 🌱 Expansion of Renewable Energy and Accelerated Carbon Neutrality Practices in Local Communities
sections.content: The expansion of renewable energy supply and local communities' efforts toward carbon neutrality are accelerating. In particular, with air-source heat officially recognized as renewable energy [27, 29, 30], the institutional foundation for expanding heat pump deployment has been established. Furthermore, in the Jeonnam region, discussions on expanding renewable energy zones to usher in an era of agri-photovoltaics are gaining full swing [24, 26, 32, 34], and the solar industry is pursuing cooperation with the Korea Energy Agency through a joint working council to achieve the 100GW renewable energy target [23]. Domestically and internationally, on-site registration for the Carbon Neutrality Point System has begun [17, 21], and various local governments and organizations, including Gwangmyeong City, Daedeok-gu, Geoje, Sejong City, and Wonju Camp Long Citizen Park, are actively practicing carbon neutrality through initiatives such as supporting carbon neutrality clubs, implementing the vehicle carbon neutrality point system, and introducing eco-friendly energy sources [11, 12, 14, 15, 18, 22].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing highlights a day where the severity of the climate crisis coexists with diverse domestic and international efforts and innovations to overcome it. This is a time when continuous attention and action become even more crucial. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.original_description: Puffins, guillemots, razorbills and terns are washing up on shores across Europe, after a string of storms affected their ability to find foodThe two puffins washed up among seaweed and bits of plastic on a beach in Newquay, Cornwall, on a damp February morning. Normally, these much-loved seabirds pull in crowds of tourists eager to see their courtship rituals, but these were rolling in the surf, dead.
